subject: LU-3289 osp: osp_precreate_thread does not check for errors

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message:
Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/36239/

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/36239/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

This patch could be split out of this series, and could likely land earlier.

Patch Set 1:

do you want to stop precreation on this OST until MDT restart? in theory ptlrpc_request_alloc() can fail due to lack of memory (which might be transition state) ?

Patch Set 1:

OSP may appear during MDT run. also, the connections OSP makes are asynchronous. usually "mount /dev/xxx /mnt/mds" has been finished already when OSP becoming connected. i.e. it's late to return an error to the mount process.
I'm not quite sure what is expected behavior here.

Patch Set 1:

But this causes it to break for any reason and not just for a fatal reason like a missing key. I think that these cases need to be sorted out.

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

PPC64 Builder: build succesful

Patch Set 1:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

The goal here was to return on failure.  If it's too late to return to the mount command then there probably needs to be messages printed to the console to report the errors.  Either way if you don't have the key loaded for the GSS to work I guess it could always be added after you notice the console messages to fix.  Do you think that is sufficient?

Patch Set 1:

When using GSS it's possible for the MDT to fail to connect to the OST due to a key error.  In that case we need to break out of the loop and report the error.  It looks like this is async as I look a little closer so its possible this can't even be reported to userspace because the mount is already completed?  If that's the case then I'm not sure the right way but if the MDT can't connect there needs to be a graceful way to fail.
Uploaded patch set 1.

Alex, can you please comment on my analysis?  I think the "inside" check & break is OK, but not the one in the main loop, which I think would result in the precreate thread exiting on any error and the OST would no longer create new objects?

(1 comment)

I'd think that we must get an error before statfs RPC? like on a conenct RPC ?

parents.subject: LU-3289 gss: Add Shared key and GSS Null functionality

commit.subject: LU-3289 osp: osp_precreate_thread does not check for errors
commit.message:
LU-3289 osp: osp_precreate_thread does not check for errors

If servers do not have a valid key for GSS the call to
osp_statfs_update() can fail.  This patch breaks the loop in the
event of a failure from osp_statfs_update().

Signed-off-by: Jeremy Filizetti <jeremy.filizetti@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I576cff86d285c4263e8d98cccb9b3c6943af6f75
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17598
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
